publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-10-19 18:17:57 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Films and videos of Cornell University football games.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Dan Sterner, August 2006; Evan Fay Earle, July 2007, December 2008

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Films and videos of Cornell University football games.
Also, "The Big Red Machine: Exciting play-by-play highlights of Cornell's 1971 Ivy League Championship season narrated by Jay Levine."
Digitization of films can be arranged if requested.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Some films labeled "highlights" may refer to a single game.
University of Pennsylvania Archives has film of all Penn-Cornell games.
See also Cornell University Football Game Highlights, #40-2-1000, for some 1950 and 1951 reels. Also Hunter Ripley Rawlings Papers, #3-13-2843, for 1991 highlights.
See also Howard Schuck papers, #37-5-2629, for more information on the team, particularly on the Ohio game of 1939.
See also Cornell University Museum Items, #47-7-1948.
See also Big Red Band Records, #37-6-3112.
Ernest William Schoder papers, #16-4-1156 for some 1929-1930 games.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University football motion pictures, #40-2-2205. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Digitization of films can be arranged upon request.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid. Use this link to see the list of all the films, over 1000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note that most films were used for practice, not broadcast. Some films were recorded by position following this pattern: reels labeled 1 = Offense, 2 = Defense, 3 = Special Teams. Other times reel numbers indicate quarters and show all plays.

did.unittitle: The Big Red Machine: Exciting play-by-play highlights of Cornell's 1971 Ivy League Championship season narrated by Jay Levine, LP record. Features Ed Marinaro.
Videos See above link for all films.
DVDs Available of the following. See above link for all films.
Cornell vs. Dartmouth, 1928 (by Allan H. Morgensen)
Cornell vs. Alfred, 1936 2 of 2 (Box 22, Reel #544) Nice shots of Schoellkopf stadium crescent crowd
Cornell vs. Syracuse, 1939, part 1 (Box 11, Reel #310) color
Cornell vs. Syracuse, 1939, part 2 (Box 11, Reel #308) color
Cornell vs. Ohio, 1939, (Reel #311, #312, #309, transferred from V-67) Shows marching band
Cornell vs. Ohio, 1939, shorter alternate version of above

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows Cornell Big Red marching band performance and tearing down the field goal at end.

did.unittitle: Cornell vs. Ohio State, 1940, part 1 (Box 11, Reel #319) color

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes photos of the players and team roster. Amazing panning shots show a packed to capacity Schoellkopf stadium and you can see the cheerleaders and marching bands perform at halftime as well. Some of the footage in the middle is out of order.Some of the footage is out of order in the middle.

did.unittitle: Cornell vs. Ohio State, 1940, part 2 (Box 11, Reel #320) color
Cornell vs. Dartmouth, 1940, fifth down play
Cornell vs. Lafayette, 1942, part 1 of 1 (Box 18 Reel #514)
Cornell vs. Lafayette, 1942, part 2 of 2 (Box 18 Reel #515)
Cornell vs. Lafayette, 1942, part 3 of 3 (Box 18 Reel #516)
Cornell vs. Princeton, 1946, parts 1-3 (Box 21, Reel #632, 634, 635, V-63)
Cornell vs. Brown, 1957 (3 Reels, originals held by Brown University)
Cornell vs. Brown, 1958 (3 Reels, originals held by Brown University)
Cornell Football Highlights, 1979, color and sound, includes clip of honoring the 1939 and 1940 Cornell team at halftime of the Yale game.
Cornell vs. Penn, 1988 (Box 28, Reel #809) 1st Half Offense
Cornell vs. Penn, 1988 (Box 28, Reel #810) 2nd Half Offense
Cornell vs. Penn, 1988 (Box 28, Reel #811)1st Half Defense
Cornell vs. Penn, 1988 (Box 28, Reel #812) 2nd Half Defense
Cornell vs. Penn, 1988 (Box 28, Reel #813) Special Teams
Cornell at. Lehigh, 1995, Special Teams (Box 51, Tape 1366)
Cornell at. Brown, 1995, Special Teams (Box 51, Tape 1373)
Cornell vs. Yale, 1995, Special Teams (Box 52, Tape 1414)
Cornell vs. Columbia, Special Teams 1995 (Box 52, Tape 1420)
Cornell vs. Columbia, 1995, complete game, endzone camera (Box 52, Tape 1423)
